englishDefinitions: A military officer whose job is to train new recruits through strict discipline, physical exercises, and repetitive drills.
Informally, any strict and demanding instructor or boss.
examples: The drill sergeant woke the recruits at 5 AM every morning for physical training.
My first music teacher was like a drill sergeant — she accepted nothing less than perfection.
A good drill sergeant pushes soldiers to their limits to prepare them for real combat.
